Separate integration tests into standalone workflow with build tag (#166)

commit.message:
fix: rewrite CI/release workflows for reliability

- Simplify ci.yml: remove fragile docs-freshness check, inline Pages
  assembly instead of complex artifact normalization, reduce from 5 to
  4 jobs (test, build, docs, deploy-pages)
- Simplify release.yml: remove prepare job, use go install for Wails
  CLI instead of go run, cleaner artifact naming
- Clean up release-please.yml: static concurrency group, simplified
  conditionals
- Add Release Please badge to README
- Clarify Release Please auth instructions (repo setting vs PAT)

The release-please workflow requires one repo setting change:
Settings → Actions → General → Workflow permissions →
  'Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(sse): send initial status and reduce heartbeat for HA ingress

Send current status immediately on SSE connect so clients don't wait
for the first change event. Reduce heartbeat interval from 30s to 15s
to keep HA Supervisor ingress proxy from buffering chunks. Extract
snapshot building into _build_snapshot() helper to avoid duplication.

commit.message:
feat: TypedArray + ArrayBuffer + DataView (closes #391) (#416)

Implement the full TypedArray family of built-in objects:

- ArrayBuffer: constructor, byteLength, slice, isView
- DataView: constructor with all get/set methods for Int8..Float64 and BigInt64/BigUint64
- All 11 TypedArray constructors (Int8Array through BigUint64Array)
- TypedArray.prototype methods: at, copyWithin, entries, every, fill, filter,
  find, findIndex, findLast, findLastIndex, forEach, includes, indexOf, join,
  keys, lastIndexOf, map, reduce, reduceRight, reverse, set, slice, some,
  sort, subarray, values
- TypedArray.from() and TypedArray.of() static methods
- JsValue variants: ArrayBuffer, TypedArray, DataView
- Global registration in install_globals

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: migrate to Blacksmith runners and improve caching (#863)

## Summary

- Migrate CI workflow runners from GitHub-hosted to Blacksmith runners
for faster builds
- Keep Windows on GitHub-hosted runners (`windows-2022`) since
Blacksmith Windows is still in public beta
- Enable Flutter SDK and pub dependency caching (`cache: true`) on all
`subosito/flutter-action` steps
- Cache Android NDK, CMake, emulator, system image, and Gradle
dependencies

commit.message:
fix: address critical issues from full codebase review (#497)

* fix: address critical issues from full codebase review

- base-tree-provider: move _loading=false to finally block to prevent
  fetchAll() from getting stuck after CancellationError (#456)
- copilot-cli-builder: escape shell metacharacters ($, backticks, quotes)
  in shellQuote(), not just spaces — prevents command injection
- extension-integrations: add .catch() on async calls in config change
  debounce timers and dispose timers on deactivation
- terminal-manager: add _disposed flag, check before firing EventEmitter
  in _scheduleChange to prevent post-disposal errors
- ado-client: call res.resume() on non-200 responses to drain socket and
  prevent HTTP connection leaks
- agent-discovery: add visited-set tracking in collectAgentMdFilesRecursive
  to prevent infinite recursion on circular directory structures
- extension-discovery: add 3s max ceiling on debounced discovery to prevent
  starvation when file events fire faster than 300ms
- github-client: add console.warn logging in all catch blocks instead of
  silently swallowing errors (fetch issues, PRs, linked PRs)
- session-context: add retry limit (30) on watcher directory retry loop
  to prevent infinite retries and memory leaks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: platform-aware backslash escaping in shellQuote (CodeQL)

shellQuote now escapes backslash on Unix (where it's a shell escape char
in double quotes) but preserves it on Windows (where it's the path
separator). Fixes CodeQL 'Incomplete string escaping or encoding' alert.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: make shellQuote test platform-aware for Linux CI

CI runs on Linux where process.platform !== 'win32', so backslashes
in the test input get escaped. Updated test to expect platform-specific
output matching the production shellQuote behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add shell metacharacter injection tests for shellQuote

Add 6 tests covering dangerous shell metacharacters: dollar-sign
command substitution, backtick commands, semicolons, pipes, ampersands,
and embedded double quotes. Validates the security fix has coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix market lines backfill producing 0 rows (#347)

* Initial plan

* Fix market lines empty output: date range truncation and pregame backfill filter

Two bugs caused 0 rows during manual backfill:

1. resolve_date_range() returned (start, start) when only start_date
   was provided without end_date, limiting backfill to a single day.
   Fixed to default end to today_ref.

2. run_capture() in pregame mode filtered out all FINAL games via
   continue, dropping every row for historical dates. Fixed to
   reclassify FINAL games as "closing" captures during backfill
   (when override_date < today).

Co-authored-by: goldbondman <4745804+goldbondman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move is_backfill computation before event loop (code review feedback)

Co-authored-by: goldbondman <4745804+goldbondman@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(settings): add 3 new tabs with comprehensive settings coverage

Add Signal Weights, Advanced AI, and Risk & Simulation tabs to
EinstellungenPage, expanding from 5 to 8 tabs. New settings include:

- Signal Weights: all 20+ WeightedScoringConfig weights (core, extended,
  esoteric) with reset-to-defaults
- Advanced AI: PPO RL config (LR, gamma, epsilon, episodes), Quantum ML

commit.message:
Fix charger deadlock: resume on connected_finished, never send 0A

- Add connected_finished to CONNECTED set so algorithm handles it
- When charger stops prematurely (SOC < target), call button.resume_charging
- Never send available_current=0 unless SOC has reached target
- Low headroom: set minimum 6A on best phase instead of pausing
- Only mode 'done' (SOC ≥ target) sends 0A to truly stop

Fixes rapid cycling: connected_charging → connected_finished → deadlock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: AutoGameLauncher — headless game creation via API, no UI required

Add AutoGameLauncher.cs: BepInEx background thread waits for GameManager.Instance,
then calls CreateSinglePlayerGame() with settings built from AIConfig.json.

- GameSettings built programmatically: tribes, map size, game mode, difficulty
- PlayerDataType.LocalUser = our backend AI via AIPoller
- PlayerDataType.Bot = game's built-in AI
- AIConfig.json extended with AutoStart + AutoStartConfig (disabled by default)
- Set AutoStart=true and configure Players[] to launch a game on startup with zero UI

Usage: set AutoStart=true in AIConfig.json, run ./start-polytopia.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(orchestration): grain lifecycle, risk state mutation, ML fixes

ORCHESTRATION AUDIT 2026-07:

RiskManagementGrain:
- Fix state mutation bug: EvaluateTradeRiskAsync was tracking positions
  during evaluation (before trade actually opened). If caller decided NOT
  to open the trade, risk state became corrupted with phantom positions.
- Add ConfirmTradeOpenedAsync for explicit position recording after trade
  is actually opened (read-evaluate-then-commit pattern).

ProphecyEngineGrain:
- Fix null reminder on StopAutoAsync/IsRunningAsync: GetReminder() can
  return null instead of throwing — add null check before UnregisterReminder.

PortfolioMakerGrain:
- Same null reminder fix for StopAutoManagementAsync/IsAutoManagingAsync.

LSTM fixes (pre-existing):
- Add gradient clipping to full-batch training path
- Align direction accuracy metrics with prediction weighting

FinancialLlmSentimentService:
- Safe Azure OpenAI response parsing with TryGetProperty guards

PPOAgentService:
- Clamp action index to valid ActionDim range

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Revert "perf(useRefObjectAsForwardedRef): add dependency array to useImperativeHandle" (#7635)

audit(trading): thread safety, position sizing, Dispose robustness

Trading & Portfolio Audit fixes:
- PaperTradingService: Add GetOpenTradesSnapshot/GetAccountSnapshot thread-safe accessors
- PaperTradingService: Read CurrentBalance under _accountLock in AutoTradeFromProphecyAsync
- PaperTradingService: Add max position size cap (10% notional) to prevent extreme exposure
- PaperTradingService: Add disposed check in AutoTradeFromProphecyAsync/OpenTradeAsync
- PaperTradingService: Reject trades with zero/negative size after adjustments
- CapitalComService: Wrap Dispose semaphore cleanup in try-catch for robustness
- CapitalComWebSocketService: Wrap connectionLock Dispose in try-catch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: spec-correct error types for Test262 compliance (closes #384) (#415)

Intercept JS-catchable StatorError variants (TypeError, RangeError,
ReferenceError, SyntaxError, URIError) in the interpreter dispatch loop
and materialise them as JsValue::Error objects before routing to the
nearest try/catch handler.  Previously these errors propagated via the
Rust ? operator and bypassed JS exception handlers entirely.

Changes:
- Add stator_error_to_js_value() to convert StatorError variants into
  JsValue::Error with the correct ErrorKind
- Replace the simple ? propagation in Interpreter::run with error
  interception that checks the handler table for catch blocks
- Add tests for TypeError caught by try/catch at both bytecode and
  full-pipeline (parse-compile-run) levels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: eliminate all JsonSerializer reflection — use Utf8JsonWriter/metadata everywhere (#1078)

* perf: replace all JsonSerializer calls in RouteHandlers.cs with Utf8JsonWriter

Replace 16 JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize calls with direct
Utf8JsonWriter usage, eliminating reflection-based serialization:

- Response writes for Dictionary results use WriteJsonResponseAsync
- Anonymous type responses use inline Utf8JsonWriter
- Deserialize calls replaced with JsonDocument.Parse
- Export methods write directly to response stream
- Job status handlers use shared WriteJobSnapshot helper
- Command execution anonymous types converted to Dictionary<string, object?>
- Remove unused JsonIndented field (now unused in this file)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace JsonSerializer with Utf8JsonWriter in Host handlers

Replace all JsonSerializer.Serialize/DeserializeAsync calls in 9 Host
project files with direct Utf8JsonWriter/JsonDocument usage. This
eliminates reflection-based serialization overhead and reduces binary
size by removing JsonSerializer dependency from these hot paths.

Files changed:
- McpRouteHandler.cs (7 calls)
- TenantApiHandlers.cs (5 calls)
- Program.cs (3 calls)
- VectorApiHandlers.cs (2 calls)
- NotificationService.cs (2 calls)
- LookupApiHandlers.cs (2 calls)
- BinaryApiHandlers.cs (2 calls)
- AgentApiHandlers.cs (1 call)
- OpenApiHandler.cs (1 call)

Added JsonWriterHelper.cs with shared ToJsonString(), WriteResponseAsync()
and WriteValue() helpers that handle all primitive types, dictionaries,
and IEnumerable without reflection or source generation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace JsonSerializer with Utf8JsonWriter/JsonDocument in RouteRegistrationExtensions

Replace all JsonSerializer.Serialize() and JsonSerializer.Deserialize()
calls with direct Utf8JsonWriter/JsonDocument usage, eliminating
reflection-based serialization.

- Replace JsonSerializerOptions fields with JsonWriterOptions
- Convert all response-body writes to use Utf8JsonWriter + JsonWriterHelper
- Convert anonymous-type projections to Dictionary<string, object?> for
  WriteValue compatibility
- Replace CommandIntent deserialization with JsonDocument manual parsing
- Replace ViewDefinition deserialization with ParseViewDefinition helper
- Extract WriteViewResultJsonAsync helper for duplicate view result pattern
- Convert inline-script serialization to JsonWriterHelper.ToJsonString

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: eliminate remaining JsonSerializer reflection in Data/Runtime

Replace all JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize calls in DataScaffold,
AuditService, and RuntimeEntityCompiler with Utf8JsonWriter/JsonDocument
via shared DataJsonWriter helper. Set JsonSerializerIsReflectionEnabledByDefault
to false — zero reflection-based JSON serialization in production code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rust-ephem
repository.description: A Rust based Python Module for Calculating Spacecraft Ephemerides and Constraints

commit.message:
perf: reduce allocations in hot paths (ephemeris, tick buffer, asset grain)

- EphemerisCacheService: reuse ThreadStatic double[6] buffer in ComputeSnapshot,
  eliminating ~1.2M array allocations during 175K-iteration warm-up loop
- TickBufferService: skip LinkedCTS allocation when no flush timeout needed
  (idle state), reducing per-tick GC pressure in the processing loop
- TickBufferService: avoid unnecessary list.ToList() copy in FlushEpic by
  swapping the reference directly since the original is removed from dict
- AssetGrain: replace List<decimal> allocation in ExtractFeatures with direct
  min/max/sum computation, eliminating per-call collection allocation in
  the grain training loop (50+ iterations per batch)
- ServiceRegistration: reuse single SqlitePragmaInterceptor instance instead
  of allocating a new one per DbContextFactory registration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
